steps: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of 15w\u00b2 and 8w: both terms share a factor of w.
Verify by distributing: w \u00b7 15w = 15w\u00b2 and w \u00b7 8 = 8w. Sum is 15w\u00b2 + 8w. \u2713
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — 'w(15w + 8)': Distributing gives 15w\u00b2 + 8w, which matches the original expression exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — '8w(15w + 1)': Distributing gives 120w\u00b2 + 8w, which does not equal 15w\u00b2 + 8w. ✗
  • Choice C — '15w\u00b2(8w + 1)': Distributing gives 120w\u00b3 + 15w\u00b2, which changes both the degree and the coefficients. ✗
  • Choice D — '23(w\u00b2 + w)': Distributing gives 23w\u00b2 + 23w, which does not match; 23 is not a factor of 15w\u00b2 + 8w. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
